title: CARE Radiology & Tele-Radiology Integration
summary: End-to-end DICOM and PACS integration for CARE HMIS, connecting hospital imaging modalities to a federated OHIF Viewer.
description: Built the CARE DICOM Enabler, a Windows .NET service using fo-dicom that bridges DICOM modalities (CT, MRI, X-ray) to the CARE Django backend, validated against the DVTK emulator before real-device rollout.
Integrated DCM4CHEE as the PACS layer and the OHIF Viewer as a federated React module inside care_fe's plugin architecture.
Mapped patient-journey pain points for radiology access across rural, semi-urban, and urban public health settings, framed around FHIR DiagnosticReport, PACS, and tele-radiology workflows.
Ran gap analysis on a TeleRadiology integration SOW (OHIF Viewer, DCM4CHEE, Report Engine) to determine vendor payment readiness.

title: eAushadhi Pharmacy Integration
summary: Connected CARE's pharmacy workflows to Karnataka's government eAushadhi API, with a design built for multi-state expansion.
description: Rearchitected the pharmacy data model around a composite natural key with per-facility-date sync control records.
Implemented trigram-based fuzzy drug-name matching using Django's TrigramSimilarity to reconcile inconsistent naming across formularies.
Built a configurable parsing layer designed for multi-state rollout, with Jharkhand's DVDMS integration as the next target.
Routed integration traffic through a Cloudflare Worker proxy in front of the government API.

title: Multi-Architecture Docker Migration
summary: Converted core care-ecosystem services to amd64 + arm64 builds publishing to GHCR.
description: Migrated GitHub Actions workflows across the CARE backend, care_fe, OHIF Viewer, slapd-dcm4chee, and Superset images to build and publish multi-platform images.
Standardized the build pipeline to support both cloud (amd64) and edge/ARM deployment targets for resource-constrained facility hardware.

title: Superset Analytics on k3s
summary: Self-managed Kubernetes infrastructure for dashboarding and analytics, deployed with GitOps practices.
description: Forked and deployed Apache Superset (care-ecosystem/superset) on a k3s cluster on AWS (ap-south-1).
Configured Cloudflare DNS, an Elastic IP, and Traefik ingress for the cluster domain.
Set up ArgoCD for GitOps-style deployment and designed a separate configs repository for dashboard import/export workflows.

title: DIGIT Public Grievance Redressal (PGR)
summary: Citizen complaint and feedback module used across 450+ urban local bodies on the DIGIT platform.
description: Ported and maintained the PGR module from DIGIT OSS Urban into the DIGIT-Frontend monorepo.
Contributed to the broader DIGIT UI module ecosystem (core, common/payments, DSS dashboards).
Built a strategic presentation and analysis exploring citizen-voice integration patterns between CARE HMIS and DIGIT PGR.

title: NCG Tele-OPD Scale-Up — Manipur
summary: Technical and functional gap analysis for a tele-OPD scale-up proposal in Manipur's public health system.
description: Analyzed technology requirements and functional gaps for scaling NCG Tele-OPD across Manipur.
Evaluated where AI-assisted triage or documentation could fit into the existing tele-consultation workflow.
